replies.msg:
Q: Living in a country where you have to speak a foreign language can cause serious social problems, as well as practical problems.

To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?



A:
Some people maintains that living in a place where forcing them to use a foreign language will cause difficulties, namely social and practical problems. I am convinced that this statement is true.

To commence, using foreign language in a nation that you live can cause social difficulties. According to researches, mastering a second language is always harder than your mother-tongue. Even if you are living at that country, it may takes years for you to be familiar with the language as good as a local do, and it is very obvious for the inhabitants to notice that you are not local. Having being labelled as a ‘non-local’ or ‘foreigner’ may cause you trouble. To name but a few, some of the foreigners will be discriminated by the way they talk, or some of the locals may think that they are invading their home, making them difficult to make friends.

Moreover, it also cause obstacles practically. As I mentioned above, it takes a lot of time for the new-comers to be familiar with foreign language. In some cases, it can cause up to 5 years or more as some people have to start from scratch. Within this period, communication will be very difficult, however, in the society, communicating with others are always indispensable. Those people will face difficulties when they want to find a job, because some jobs may not be suitable for those barely speaks their language. Some of them may find it hard even when they just want to simply buy something. Misunderstanding is also not uncommon to those who are not familiar enough with foreign language.

All in all, the examples mentioned illustrates how people living in a place speaks a foreign language can seriously affect them both socially and practically. I strongly agree to this argument.

(299)

replies.msg: 個個都係咁架啦

好快scan咗次

1. 主要問題都係common subject-verb agreement, 3rd person singular

2. As I mentioned above 唔需要寫，呢句係多餘無意思

3. Some people maintains that living in a place where forcing them to use a foreign language will cause difficulties,

subject轉返做人最好，collocation就用最common嗰款encounter difficulties

4. Misunderstanding is also not uncommon to those who are not familiar enough with foreign language.

呢度就唔好用double negative啦，一向confusing同唔prefer咁寫

5. 成篇文太多those, some, them，用得太多除咗tedious亦會confusing，用其他noun代替，westerners, foreigners, non-local travellers, such ... ... , the aforementioned, the above-mentioned



自己有睇另外巴打一篇文，畀咗少少comment：

政府工officer grade 筆試 (英文篇),歡迎大家入黎交流
https://lih.kg/1027647
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區
